response.justification: Aisha brings 15+ years of broad technical depth across web, mobile, embedded, and high-performance systems, along with genuine developer tooling contributions (CI/CD infrastructure, test frameworks, MCP generators). However, this is fundamentally a people management role, and the resume contains no evidence of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, running 1:1s, or leading teams — the defining responsibilities of the position. The career trajectory is solidly senior IC; even Staff-level titles here appear to reflect technical seniority rather than organizational leadership. Without demonstrated people management experience, the candidate does not meet the minimum requirements for this role.
strengths: 15+ years of hands-on engineering experience across a wide range of domains (web, mobile, embedded, high-performance systems) exceeds the 8-year minimum.

Meaningful developer tooling contributions: built CI/CD infrastructure, authored a D-language test runner and assertion library, and recently contributed to MCP frameworks supporting agentic workflows.
Active community teaching through workshops and conference talks signals communication ability and a genuine interest in developer productivity and knowledge sharing.
concerns: No evidence of people management: the resume contains no mention of direct reports, performance conversations, hiring, or team-building — all core to this role.
Career trajectory is entirely individual contributor; even the Founder and Staff Engineer titles describe personal technical output rather than organizational leadership.
No indication of strategic roadmap ownership, cross-team alignment, or leading organizational change at any scope.

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and testing infrastructure contributions
